skill.name: tdd-workflows-tdd-cycle
skill.description: Guide strict red-green-refactor TDD cycles with test design, failing-test validation, implementation gates, refactoring, coverage checks, and review checkpoints.
skill.summary: Runs a disciplined TDD workflow from requirements to final review.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings for shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance are false positives from Markdown formatting and TDD terminology. No malicious intent, prompt injection, network access, credential access, or executable code was found in SKILL.md. A low-risk workflow concern remains because rollback guidance does not state approval or preservation requirements.

low_findings.title: Unqualified Rollback Guidance
low_findings.description: The workflow tells the agent to rollback to the last valid state when TDD discipline is broken. This is a low operational risk because it does not state that user approval and preservation of user changes are required before rollback actions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit, but security impact depends on how an agent implements rollback. No destructive command is provided in the skill.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Inline Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown inline code spans for a resource path and command-line flags. They do not execute commands and do not include shell syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each location is prose with Markdown formatting only. No executable Ruby, shell, or command substitution context exists in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are ordinary words such as description, Design, and design. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing API, encryption API, or security-sensitive comparison appears at these lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is software design guidance, not cryptography. The matches appear to be substring detections rather than actual weak algorithm use.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Reconnaissance Matches
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance locations describe normal TDD concepts such as verifying tests pass, rollback as process recovery, and anti-patterns to avoid. No system enumeration, network probing, host discovery, or scanning behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are checklist headings or test workflow text. There is no evidence of system or network reconnaissance intent.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose TDD discipline when implementation starts before meaningful tests exist. This skill provides a phased red-green-refactor workflow with checkpoints for tests, coverage, refactoring, and review.
actual_capabilities: Breaks work into requirements analysis, test design, red, green, refactor, integration, and review phases.
Defines coverage thresholds for line, branch, and critical path testing.
Provides gates for failing-test verification before implementation begins.
Guides minimal implementation after tests fail for the right reasons.
Adds refactoring checkpoints for production code and test code.
Tracks TDD metrics such as cycle count, coverage progression, and refactoring frequency.
limitations: It is a guidance-only skill and does not include executable test tooling.
Referenced specialized subagents may not exist in every Claude, Codex, or Claude Code environment.
The optional implementation playbook file is referenced but not included in this package.
Coverage thresholds may need adjustment for legacy systems or non-code tasks.

use_cases.title: Start a New Feature with TDD
use_cases.description: Use the workflow to define acceptance criteria, write failing tests, implement only what passes, and refactor safely.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Testing Practice
use_cases.description: Use the checkpoints to keep a team aligned on test-first development, coverage expectations, and review criteria.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Test Discipline
use_cases.description: Use the validation gates to identify skipped red phases, weak tests, and missing refactoring steps.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a TDD Task
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a small feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the TDD cycle skill for this feature. Define acceptance criteria, test scenarios, and the first failing test before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Run Red Green Refactor
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate execution for one feature slice.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the red-green-refactor workflow to this change. Verify the tests fail first, implement the minimum behavior, then refactor while tests stay green.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Test Suite Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a module or service.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use suite mode for this module. Design unit and integration tests, define fixtures and mocks, then set coverage gates before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Audit TDD Compliance
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this completed work against the TDD workflow. Report red phase evidence, green phase minimality, refactoring quality, coverage gaps, and corrective actions.

output_examples.input: A developer asks to add password reset support using TDD.
output: Acceptance criteria for valid, expired, reused, and missing reset tokens.
A failing-test plan that starts with token validation and notification behavior.
Green phase guidance to implement only the behavior needed by the failing tests.
Refactor checklist for naming, duplication, and fixture clarity.

output_examples.input: An engineering lead asks whether a pull request followed TDD.
output: Evidence of tests written before implementation, or a clear gap if evidence is missing.

output_examples.input: A QA engineer asks for stronger integration coverage.
output: Integration scenarios for component boundaries and data flow.
Failure expectations for the initial red phase.
Validation gates for passing tests and stable coverage.
Metrics to track after the new tests are added.

best_practices: Keep each red-green-refactor cycle small enough to verify quickly.
Record why each failing test fails before writing production logic.
Refactor production code and tests only while the full relevant suite stays green.
anti_patterns: Starting implementation before a failing test exists.
Changing tests only to force a green result.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It guides a structured TDD workflow from requirements through final review.

faq.question: Does it run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides process guidance and checkpoints, but the user or agent must run the project test commands.

faq.question: Can I use it for existing code?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide new tests, integration coverage, and review of whether recent work followed TDD.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should I prepare before using it?
faq.answer: Prepare the feature goal, acceptance criteria, test command, coverage target, and any project testing conventions.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk level low?
faq.answer: Static security alerts were false positives, but rollback guidance should be clarified before sensitive repository work.
